1960s.title: Development decade and controlled prices
1960s.body: Pakistan's 1960s growth period mixed industrial policy, administered prices, and agriculture-linked household costs.
1960s.event: Industrialization push and Green Revolution spillovers
1960s.outcome: The rupee's purchasing power begins from a relatively low index base.
1960s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

1970s.title: Oil shock, nationalization, and fiscal strain
1970s.body: The 1970s brought political reset, nationalization, oil-price shocks, and fiscal pressure, all of which affected consumer prices.
1970s.driver: Fuel imports, fiscal deficits, food prices
1970s.outcome: Energy and government finance became important inflation channels.

1980s.title: Remittances and managed adjustment
1980s.body: Migration remittances, aid flows, and managed exchange-rate policy shaped household incomes and import costs during the 1980s.
1980s.driver: Exchange rate, imported goods, money growth
1980s.outcome: Purchasing power moved with both domestic prices and external financing.
1980s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

1990s.title: IMF programs and rupee pressure
1990s.body: Fiscal deficits, IMF programs, energy pricing, and exchange-rate pressure made inflation a recurring policy issue in the 1990s.
1990s.outcome: Older rupee values lost purchasing power through repeated adjustment cycles.

2000s.title: Commodity shock after a growth boom
2000s.body: Growth, credit expansion, and then the 2007-2008 food and oil shock pushed inflation sharply into everyday budgets.
2000s.outcome: The decade converted global commodity stress into domestic price pressure.
2000s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

2010s.title: Energy reform and stabilization attempts
2010s.body: Electricity pricing, IMF-backed stabilization, and exchange-rate management shaped inflation as Pakistan tried to contain external imbalances.
2010s.driver: Tariffs, taxes, exchange rate, food prices
2010s.outcome: Inflation eased in some years, but structural pressure remained.

2020s.title: Floods, currency stress, and food inflation
2020s.body: The pandemic, import-price pressure, rupee depreciation, energy adjustments, and the 2022 floods made purchasing power a national crisis issue.
2020s.outcome: The 2024 endpoint reflects an especially steep early-2020s price climb.
